question.stem: A museum education team is creating an interactive exhibit planner and finds a bug that only appears with empty input. What is the best next testing action?
choices: Assume users will not submit empty input
Add boundary test cases including empty and minimum inputs
question.explanation: Boundary-focused testing catches edge-case failures early. A common wrong choice is "Test only average cases" because it sounds plausible but does not address the core condition. Takeaway: identify the exact requirement before selecting an approach.
